steps.description: Click the close (X) button at the top-right corner of the "Welcome to VS Code" sign-in dialog to dismiss the sign-in prompt in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "File" menu icon located at the top-left corner of the Visual Studio Code window, adjacent to the application menu bar.
Click the "New Window" option in the File menu of Visual Studio Code, highlighted and located below "New File..." with the shortcut "Ctrl+Shift+N" visible.
Click the File menu in the top-left corner of the Visual Studio Code window to open the application menu.
Click on the "Open Folder..." menu item in the File menu of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Open" button in the bottom right corner of the file chooser dialog to confirm folder selection in a desktop application.
Click the “New File” button in the VS Code Explorer sidebar to create a new file in the current workspace.
@assertion there's "Build a Declarative Agent" "View Samples" buttons exist.
Click the "English" language indicator button in the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window, next to the minimize and maximize controls, to change the display language.
Click the "Source Control" icon in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code interface to open the source control panel.
Click the "Create a New Agent App" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome pane within Visual Studio Code, indicated by the blue button in the left sidebar.
Click the "Declarative Agent | Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dialog within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "No Action" option in the "Add an action to your declarative agent" dropdown within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the Workspace Folder dialog of Visual Studio Code to select /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects as the project root directory.
Type text 'test' into the "Input an application name" text field in the command palette at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input "test" in the "Input an application name" text field within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Close (Ctrl+W)" button on the "Preview README.md" tab in Visual Studio Code to close the markdown preview.
Click the "M365" icon in the Visual Studio Code activity bar to open the Microsoft 365 extension panel.
Click the "Create N Development" button within the DEVELOPMENT section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT side panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Get Started" link under the "HELP AND FEEDBACK" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar of Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's "Build a Declarative Agent" doc opened.
@assertion "HELP AND FEEDBACK" sections contain: Documentation, Get Started, Report Issues on Github
